summary: Credential harvester and remote-shell backdoor exfiltrating to sentry.citadel-casino.com
details: The package's CLI (bin/scan-only.js) performs two distinct attacker-benefit operations against any user who runs it. (1) collectRecon() reads installer-owned secrets — ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.npmrc, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.gitconfig, shell histories (.bash_history,.zsh_history, PSReadline), full process.env, plus network/process state — and POSTs the bundle to the hardcoded endpoint https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/collect with a hardcoded x-api-key header. (2) When invoked with --diagnose, the CLI fetches https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/decoy, extracts a hidden command via first-letter-of-each-word steganography (with an 'endofpayload' marker and an unescape map turning tokens like 'sbslash'/'sdollar'/'sspace' back into shell metacharacters), and passes the decoded string to execSync on /bin/sh (Linux/macOS) or cmd.exe (Windows) — a remote-shell backdoor with no integrity check, controlled entirely by whoever serves /decoy. The collector hostname impersonates the Sentry observability brand to look benign. Both behaviors fire on user invocation of the CLI (npx scan-only / scan-only --diagnose), exfiltrating credentials and granting the operator of sentry.citadel-casino.com arbitrary shell execution on the victim host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
